{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.3,

    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus on (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity, or (B) the technical/argument consistency and whether the empirical numbers support the theoretical claims?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The paragraph makes specific theoretical claims about curvature-dependent guarantees, so verifying the internal logic and interpretation is critical.",
    "R1.3": "It presents empirical curvature estimates and optimality ratios to support a narrative, so we must assess whether the reported numbers substantiate the claims.",
    "RX.1": "Some judgments may depend on unstated assumptions (e.g., how curvature is estimated), so uncertainty must be explicitly flagged.",
    "RX.2": "We should avoid endorsing stronger conclusions than the text and cited results warrant.",
    "RX.3": "The task is to project responsibilities without rewriting or extending the paragraph’s content.",
    "RX.4": "Care is needed to avoid reinforcing potentially misleading technical interpretations that could propagate in scholarship.",
    "RX.5": "All assessments will be grounded solely in the provided text and standard, widely cited results to ensure traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity, or (B) the technical/argument consistency and whether the empirical numbers support the theoretical claims?

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph makes specific theoretical claims about curvature-dependent guarantees, so verifying the internal logic and interpretation is critical.
rationale.R1.3: It presents empirical curvature estimates and optimality ratios to support a narrative, so we must assess whether the reported numbers substantiate the claims.
rationale.RX.1: Some judgments may depend on unstated assumptions (e.g., how curvature is estimated), so uncertainty must be explicitly flagged.
rationale.RX.2: We should avoid endorsing stronger conclusions than the text and cited results warrant.
rationale.RX.3: The task is to project responsibilities without rewriting or extending the paragraph’s content.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid reinforcing potentially misleading technical interpretations that could propagate in scholarship.
rationale.RX.5: All assessments will be grounded solely in the provided text and standard, widely cited results to ensure traceability.
